question: Write a C++ program that prompts the user to enter the length and width of a rectangle (as floating-point numbers), then calculates and displays its area and perimeter with appropriate labels.

test_cases.expected:
Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Rectangle Properties ---
Length:    10.50
Width:     6.00
Area:      63.00
Perimeter: 33.00

Enter the length of the rectangle: Invalid input. Please enter a positive floating-point number for length.
Enter the length of the rectangle: Invalid input. Please enter a positive floating-point number for length.
Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: Invalid input. Please enter a positive floating-point number for width.
Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Rectangle Properties ---
Length:    7.00
Width:     3.00
Area:      21.00
Perimeter: 20.00

Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Rectangle Properties ---
Length:    1.23
Width:     4.56
Area:      5.61
Perimeter: 11.58

driver:
int main() { float length, width; while (true) { std::cout << "Enter the length of the rectangle: "; std::cin >> length; if (std::cin.fail() || length <= 0) { std::cout << "Invalid input. Please enter a positive floating-point number for length.
"; std::cin.clear(); std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '
'); } else { break; } } while (true) { std::cout << "Enter the width of the rectangle: "; std::cin >> width; if (std::cin.fail() || width <= 0) { std::cout << "Invalid input. Please enter a positive floating-point number for width.
"; std::cin.clear(); std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '
'); } else { break; } } float area = length * width; float perimeter = 2 * (length + width); std::cout << "
--- Rectangle Properties ---
"; std::cout << "Length:    " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << length << std::endl; std::cout << "Width:     " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << width << std::endl; std::cout << "Area:      " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << area << std::endl; std::cout << "Perimeter: " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << perimeter << std::endl; return 0; }
